children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary may make a performance award in connection with a grant made, on or after , to an eligible recipient for a project under section 3141 or 3149 of this title.October 27, 2004

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 1 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate regulations to establish performance measures for making performance awards under subsection (a).

children.label: A
children.text: whether the recipient meets or exceeds scheduling goals;

children.label: B
children.text: whether the recipient meets or exceeds job creation goals;

children.label: C
children.text: amounts of private sector capital investments leveraged; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other factors as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall base the amount of a performance award made under subsection (a) in connection with a grant on the extent to which a recipient meets or exceeds performance measures established in connection with the grant.

children.heading: Maximum amount
children.label: 2 Maximum amount
children.text: The amount of a performance award may not exceed 10 percent of the amount of the grant.

children.heading: Use of awards
children.label: d Use of awards
children.text: A recipient of a performance award under subsection (a) may use the award for any eligible purpose under this chapter, in accordance with and such regulations as the Secretary may promulgate.section 3212 of this title

children.heading: Federal share
children.label: e Federal share
children.text: Notwithstanding , the funds of a performance award may be used to pay up to 100 percent of the cost of an eligible project or activity.section 3144 of this title

children.heading: Treatment in meeting non-Federal share requirements
children.label: f Treatment in meeting non-Federal share requirements
children.text: For the purposes of meeting the non-Federal share requirements under this, or any other, Act the funds of a performance award shall be treated as funds from a non-Federal source.

children.heading: Terms and conditions
children.label: g Terms and conditions
children.text: In making performance awards under subsection (a), the Secretary shall establish such terms and conditions as the Secretary considers to be appropriate.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: h Funding
children.text: The Secretary shall use any amounts made available for economic development assistance programs to carry out this section.

children.heading: Reporting requirement
children.label: i Reporting requirement
children.text: The Secretary shall include information regarding performance awards made under this section in the annual report required under .section 3213 of this title

children.heading: Review
children.label: 1 Review
children.text: The Comptroller General shall regularly review the implementation of this section.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 2 Report
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Comptroller General shall submit to the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate and the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives a report on the findings of the Comptroller on implementation of this subsection.October 27, 2004
